app.name: CargoPilot
app.tagline: From material list to transport documents

dashboard.privacy: CargoPilot does not store history, pasted data or document files on the server. After download everything is cleared from memory.

login.title: Sign in
login.setup: No admin configured. Set ADMIN_USERNAME, ADMIN_EMAIL and ADMIN_PASSWORD.

wizard.adjustTotalWeightHint: All line weights are scaled proportionally.
wizard.dgIntro: Complete the dangerous goods details for all packages marked as DG or in which a UN number was detected.
wizard.dgSource: UN data can be fetched automatically from the ADR database (FreightUtils).
wizard.dgVehicleHelp: Description of the vehicle or load for this DG position.
wizard.dgIncluded: Dangerous goods completed for {{count}} package(s); the data is included in the selected DG documents.

review.intro: Enter a description per package: pick equipment from the catalogue or type your own description with dimensions. Paste from Excel via Import.
review.descriptionPlaceholder: Pick equipment or type e.g. steel angle 80x80x8x6000
review.noEquipmentMatch: No catalog match — free description will be used
review.noCatalogMatch: No catalog match — free description will be used
review.noLines: Add at least one package with a description.
review.importHint: Paste copied columns from Excel (description | quantity | unit), or download the template and upload a file.

messages.dg_un_detected: UN number detected — package marked as dangerous goods

materieel.intro: Manage your own equipment database. Download the template, fill in items and import. Recognized names are matched in the wizard.
materieel.search: Search by name, SAP code or alias…
materieel.importHint: Download the template first, fill in your equipment, then upload the file (.xlsx, .csv or .txt). Existing items are updated by SAP code or specifications.

settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: Customize the app appearance and language.
settings.themeHint: Choose light, dark, or follow system settings.
settings.autoDetectNote: In the wizard, the input language of your material list is always detected automatically.

modality.title: Choose your transport modality
modality.intro: Start a new shipment by choosing a modality. Then select which forms to prepare and enter your articles, products or equipment.
modality.roadDesc: CMR, ADR document and internal forms
modality.railDesc: CIM consignment note with RID data
modality.seaDesc: B/L instructions, VGM and IMO DG form
modality.inlandDesc: ADN document, VGM and internal forms
modality.airDesc: AWB instructions and IATA DG declaration

forms.title: Which forms do you want to prepare?
forms.intro: Select the documents for this shipment. Only forms relevant to the chosen modality are shown; multimodal offers all forms.
forms.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one form to continue.

docfields.title: Shipment and document details
docfields.intro: Shared data is reused across all selected documents. Fields marked * are required for export. Carrier data and operational fields stay empty until known.
docfields.confirmExplicit: I deliberately confirm these declarations on behalf of the responsible person

wizardDocs.title: Documents
wizardDocs.intro: Download the selected documents. The status shows whether a document is ready for export.
wizardDocs.dgBlocked: Dangerous goods classification is incomplete. Fill in UN number, Proper Shipping Name, class and packaging details in the dangerous goods step.
wizardDocs.exportNotice: Note: generated documents are drafts. Verify, complete and sign by an authorised person before use. See the disclaimer.

legal.title: Disclaimer and licence
legal.intro: CargoPilot is a tool that prepares transport and accompanying documents based on your input. It does not provide legal, customs or safety advice. Please read the terms below carefully; by using CargoPilot you accept them.

sections.heading: 1. Draft — not a final document
sections.body: Every document produced by CargoPilot is a draft. Before use, hand-over, submission or signature it must be fully verified, completed and signed by a duly authorised and qualified person. Signature fields, carrier data and operational fields are deliberately not pre-filled.

sections.heading: 2. Dangerous goods
sections.body: Classification, packaging, marking, labelling and documentation of dangerous goods (ADR, RID, ADN, IMDG, IATA DGR) are the exclusive responsibility of the shipper and other legally designated parties. CargoPilot never automatically turns a free-text description into a final classification and does not replace certified master data, a safety adviser or the current, authorised regulations.

sections.heading: 3. No warranties (AS IS)
sections.body: The software and all generated documents are provided "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE", without any express or implied warranty, including warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, accuracy, timeliness, completeness or non-infringement. No warranty is given that reference data or form templates are correct, current or complete.

sections.heading: 4. Limitation of liability
sections.body: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the author(s), rights holder(s) and contributors of CargoPilot exclude all liability for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, punitive or consequential damages — including loss of profit, business interruption, fines, penalties, delays, seized or refused shipments, loss of or damage to goods, environmental damage, personal injury or loss of data — arising out of or in connection with the use of, or inability to use, the application or the generated documents, on any legal basis and even if advised of the possibility of such damage. You use CargoPilot entirely at your own risk.

sections.heading: 5. Indemnification
sections.body: You indemnify the author(s) and rights holder(s) of CargoPilot against all third-party claims arising out of or in connection with your use of the application or the generated documents.

sections.heading: 6. Licence — Apache License 2.0 with Commons Clause
sections.body: CargoPilot is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, supplemented by the Commons Clause. Under the Apache License 2.0 the software is provided without warranties and with a limitation of liability (sections 7 and 8 of that licence). Use within your own organisation is permitted; selling, reselling, hosting as a paid service or otherwise commercially redistributing the software itself requires prior written permission from the rights holder (Commons Clause). The full text is in the repository's LICENSE file.

sections.heading: 7. Governing law
sections.body: This disclaimer is governed by Dutch law, to the extent not precluded by mandatory law. If any provision is held void or unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in full force.

compliance.title: Dangerous goods compliance check
compliance.intro: Automatic checks for {{profiles}} based on your input.
compliance.incompleteHint: Enter the ADR transport category and total quantity for: {{products}}. Use the UN lookup or ADR Table A column 15.
compliance.category0Hint: Transport category 0 ({{products}}): carriage under the 1.1.3.6 exemption is not permitted; all ADR provisions apply in full.
compliance.aboveThresholdHint: Above 1000 points, orange plates, an ADR driver training certificate, instructions in writing and tunnel restrictions apply, among other things.
compliance.noSegregationIssues: No mixed-loading or segregation conflicts found within the entered positions.
compliance.qTitle: IATA Q value ('all packed in one')
compliance.caoHint: Cargo Aircraft Only applies to: {{products}}. State this on the Shipper's Declaration and the AWB handling information.
compliance.disclaimer: These checks are guidance based on ADR 2025 and IATA DGR 67th edition and do not replace a safety adviser or the official regulations. See the disclaimer.
